feed.title: SAP Community - Settlement Management
feed.subtitle: Settlement Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP_Condition Contract_Spreading fixed amount accrual according to the number of days
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

i have an yearly fixed amount to be accrued on a monthly base proportionally to the number of days of each month. So, considering for instance 1.200€ yearly, i don't have to post 100 € each month but according to the number of days of each month; below an example.

Yearly bonus: 1.200 €

Delta accrual on 31.01 = (1.200/365)*31 --> 101,92 €

Delta accrual on 28.02 = (1.200/365)*28 --> 92,05 €

Delta accrual on 31.03 = (1.200/365)*31 --> 101,92 €

Delta accrual on 30.04 = (1.200/365)*30 --> 98,63 €

etc...

Do you know how i can manage it?

Thanks and regards

Pier Paolo

entry.title: CCM Delta accruals calculation
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question about delta accruals. I created an invoice 1, a contract, invoice 2 and did a partial settlement. When i run a delta accrual, the partial settlement, the delta accruals for invoice 1 are calculated correctly but in the settlement document also a line is created for an accrual that reverses the Partial settlement.

There should be no accrual for the Partial settlement done, I am using standard configuration. Anyone can tell me how to correct the delta accrual calculation so that no accruals are done Partial settlements?

 

Thanks

entry.title: WBS to AuC - Only settling to one Dep Area (01) (0L), I want it to post to both 0L and 2L.
entry.content: Solved

entry.title: SAP TPM-CCM : Over Accrual to the customer G/L
entry.content:
Hi,

We have SAP TPM system integrated with S/4 Condition Contract Management. The CRM Claim management is used for Settlements. I am looking for guidance to handle the over accruals through the claims.

When the Accrual value from SD Billing document against the Condition contract is 5,000 USD.

An invoice claim is created for 7,000 USD. The claim is approved, Settlement generated and replicated to S/4. Allowing claims having excess amount is a business requirement. The system shows a warning while approval for amount greater than accrual.

Current Behavior : The S/4 settlement document generates the Accounting document, where the entire amount is updated against the Customer G/L.

Expected Behavior : In the accounting document, the accrual amount should be posted to the Customer G/L, and the remaining amount should be posted to another G/L.

entry.title: CCM : Condition REA5 triggered during partial settlement without having accruals posted beforehand
entry.content:
Hello,

when there are negative settlement items in a partial settlement document (due to a negative business volume on these items and the split criteria implemented), on some items REA5 is being triggered. For this condition contract no accruals have been posted beforehand.  REA5 should not be triggered in this case as it is normally triggered when the accrual posted is larger then the rebate amount during settlement.

Pricing procedures are created in reference with the sap standard ones. 

Anyone has experience how to deal with negative settlement items? 

In order to simplify the example I took a contract with only negative items:

testcase: contract with 15% on both accrual and rebate condition type

WB2R_BUSVOL: a G2 and S1 billing document

 

only a partial settlement is executed which results in two items

item 1 has a positive business volume , this relates to the S1 billing doc 

item 2 has a negative business volume amount which relates to G2 . Here Z1A5 is triggered but it should not be triggered. Anyone an idea what is the logic when REA5(=  z1a5) is triggered by the settlement program?

 Fact that the business volume amount on the first example is negative is not the reason for the issue as for another contract for the same business volume, the Z1A5 was triggered on the first item and not on the second item.

Thanks in advance for some hints,

br, Ilse

entry.title: Condition Contract: Accrual capping, Intercompany postings possibility
entry.content:
Hi,

I have three question for conditions contract process (Settlement Management)

1. I have requirement to put capping for accrual. e.g Accruals should not happen once a specific amount is reached. Is it possible in standard?

2. Does intercompany settlement works under condition contracts? e.g: I have three companies A, B and C. Company C is selling goods to end customer where Company A and B would receive some commission. My approach is to set up separate condition contract under agent commission for A and B. At the time of settlement Company C will issue the settlement document but at the same time company  A and B should also post the incoming document against the company C settlement document like Intercompany supplier invoice happens with IDOC.

3. There is requirement that customer would issue and debit memo for rebate amount and system should post that incoming document against the condition contract as settlement document.is it possible?

entry.title: Quantity Scales determination in the multi payee condition contract
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In case of Multi Payee Condition Contract (0S02), the requirement is to determine the scale for settlement condition type is based on the Total Business quantity for all the customers maintained in the Business volume criteria in the condition contract, however the settlement calculation will be done on the basis of individual customer business volume quantity.

In this case, only the scales need to be determined based on the total business volume quantity. It works for the total business volume (Amount) when we assign Routine 2 to RES1 condition type and RBVT condition type to the business volume criteria. However, it does not work in cases where the scales need to be determined based on the total quantity.

Please suggest.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: Multiple Profit center issue in Delta accrual document
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When we are performing Delta accruals against the condition contract for multiple profit centers, the Delta accruals document is not being posted to the accounts. An error message "GLT2201 - Balancing field Profit Center in line item not filled" is displayed. The settlement document is created correctly for the multiple profit centers and the invoices are also posted correctly for different profit centers. However, when we execute the Delta accruals document, the system is unable to post the document for the multiple profit centers. The Delta accruals work fine in the case of a single profit center.

Please advise.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: Output Idoc for Settlement document
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

I am trying to issue an idoc from a settlement document. I have check the noteRaising EDI / IDoc message from Agency Business / Settlement Management, which is not helpful.

Which output type (for application WR) should i use and what settings need to be done ? is there any relevant documentation/note/link about this ?

thank you all in advance.

BR,

Stratos

entry.title: Requirement 77 (CCS: Accr from Settl) assigned to the Condition type REA5
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In the Standard Pricing procedure for Settlement A10005 (Rebates - Germany), Requirement 77 (CCS: Accr from Settl) is assigned to the REA5 condition type. However, in the pricing procedure A17005 (Rebates US), it is not assigned to the condition type REA5. Therefore, I would like to understand the functionality or purpose of the routine 77 assigned to REA5. If it's not assigned, what are the implications?

Please advise.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: Settlement Management Accrual Reversal RCA2 Issue
entry.content:
Hi,

when i create a partial settlement it is somehow fetching a greater amount for accrual reversal for all the items. any idea what could be causing this. in the Business vol config all the CDS views are maintained for accrual reversal.

entry.title: Problem: Multiple Freight Settlement creation in SAP TM
entry.content:
For 1 freight order the system creates automatically different freight settlements to different dates automatically and that is not correct.

It should be that for 1 Freight order the system creates only 1 freight settlement document.

The steps are following in the system:

When departed it makes automatic goods issue of the delivery note.

Furthermore 1 Freight settlement is automatically created.

When the GI is registered then 1 correct freight settlement is automatically created (via JOBPLANER)

This example was correct.

But suddenly to a later point for example 03.01.2024 or 28.12.2023 a further freight settlement is automatically created. And that is not correct.

The yellow marked freight settlement documents were created automatically without any change in the system.

Here are some Screenshots:

 

I checked already the customizing and the master data I did not find an issue in these areas.

Is there maybe an SAP Note? Or is it there anything in Coding ABAP?

How is it possible to solve this problem?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: After Set TECO status i face error in cost transfer AUC to Fix asset
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Please tell me correct  Settlement Scenario .

1st i Set TECO status , i face error in cost transfer AUC to Fix asset

After remove TECO Status  cost successfully  transfer AUC to fix asset 

Please find Attached Error Screenshot

entry.title: CCM: How add field to table VWB2_VBRKVBRP_1
entry.content:
Hi,

We need the field VSBED to be added to business volume table VWB2_VBRKVBRP_1 because we get an error when running tx WB2R_BUSVOL. The field has already been added to structure WB2_S_BVB_FIELDCAT. Please advise how to get field VSBED in the business volume table.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Settlement calculation on the basis of Business volume customer for Single Payee contract
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I have a scenario where the condition contract is created for a different customer (A) in the header (to settle the accruals against) single payee, and a different customer (B) in the Business Volume table (to post the accruals against). When we check the line items for the accruals from billing documents, the line item is recorded against B, which is correct as this is the customer from the BV table and we also maintained the condition record for the customer (B). However, when the delta accruals are posted or settlement documents are posted, the system is not able to derive the customer from the business volume table, and hence the condition record is not determining for customer (B). Instead, customer (A) is determining, and for customer (A), there is no record in the system, resulting in the documents being created with zero value.

Is there a configuration setting to adjust this so that customer (B) will determine and find the condition record?

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: Open items check at settelment document creation
entry.content: We have a business requirement: when running settlement for condition contracts, the system should check customer open items. If there are any due invoices, the system should not allow the creation of the settlement document.

entry.title: No calculation quantity and net value in condition contract
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

i created a condition contract but i don't display quantity, net amount and other value.

Can you help me?

Thank you.

entry.title: TECO status
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

1. Please tell me the sequence of settlement scenarios for the capital project.

2. After TECO status, is a good issue possible or not?

I checked the system status (BS23). Good movement is allowed, but when i create good issue against reservation I face some issues.

Please Find attached Screenshots.

entry.title: How to replace expired condition contract with an active condition contract?
entry.content: I wanted to address a recurring issue that our business team has encountered regarding returns credit note and their impact on our System. At times, our business team creates return orders against the billing documents that were generated at least two years ago. In these cases, the System automatically determines the expired condition contracts from the original billing documents to the newly created return order and subsequently to return credit note. Also, as these return credit notes gets settled in the previous month under the new condition contracts (using t-code: WB2R_SC), we have been observing a discrepancy. Requesting you to let us know, if there is any BAdI or a User Exit that we can make use of to resolve this issue.

entry.title: External Commission Settlement of Invoices created directly from sales orders prior to delivery
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

        We want to use condition contracts for payment of external commissions using condition contract technique to dealers. Business requirement mandates that no settlement should be done (accrual or actual payment) unless a delivery has been created. For sales from stock products we do not have an issue as billing always takes place post delivery. But for custom make to orders, we have order related billing and billing document is created much ahead of the delivery. However, since condition contract settlement gets triggered by billing, system doesnt check if the delivery for the custom order has been made or not. Is there any way to achieve this technically? I have looked for a BADI under Settlement process which allows us to cancel the settlement run for an entire contract if one of the invoices in the business volume is found to be without any delivery. But customer doesnt want this to happen- we need to skip only that particular invoice and settle the commissions for the remaining invoices where delivery has been made for the custom orders.

Please share your opinion on how we can achieve this - specifically, if there is any BADI which allows us the scope to remove the invoices (without delivery) from the business volume for calculating the settlement amount.

Thanks

Anustup Chakrabarti
